evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Implement testing and data validation processes within dbt; design and maintain analytical data models with focus on traceability and quality
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Classified as rigour because responsibilities centre on testing, data validation, traceability, quality, and standardising metrics — with no velocity or speed-to-ship signals present.
evidence.domain_risk: healthcare digitalisation; over 10 million patients across Europe, LATAM, the United States, and the Middle East
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Classified as high because Top Doctors Group operates in regulated healthcare serving 10M+ patients, placing it in the default-high sector despite the marketplace/platform model.
evidence.collaboration_width: Data Team Lead; Senior Data Engineer
evidence.data_team_maturity: work closely with the Data Team Lead, Senior Data Engineer
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Classified as mid because named data roles (Data Team Lead, Senior Data Engineer) exist and the task is to evolve and scale the existing analytical layer, not build from zero.
evidence.jd_authorship: Design and maintain analytical data models in dbt with focus on traceability and quality; Implement testing and data validation processes within dbt
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Classified as hiring_manager because specific tools (dbt, BigQuery, Tableau) appear in responsibilities with precise application context (traceability, validation, KPI standardisation).
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: define, standardise, and document metrics and KPIs; ensure consistent, actionable information organisation-wide
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Classified as internal_data because the primary output is organisation-wide metric standardisation and analytics layer governance — serving internal data consumers across the organisation.
evidence.autonomy_level: Define, standardise, and document metrics and KPIs; collaborate with non-technical teams to translate business needs
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Classified as mixed because 'define and standardise' signals strategic ownership of the metrics layer while 'collaborate to translate business needs' signals execution in service of stakeholders.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No AI tool usage or AI infrastructure enablement is mentioned anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: Implement testing and data validation processes within dbt
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Classified as responsibility because 'implement testing and data validation processes' is an ownership verb framing the candidate as accountable for the testing practice, not just familiar with the tool.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: traceability and quality; reliable metrics; testing and data validation
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Classified as moderate because data reliability and quality are named concerns throughout, but the framing is delivery-oriented with no compliance, audit, or trust-failure language.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: designing and evolving the analytical layer used across the organisation
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Classified as fix_scale because the role is to 'evolve' an existing analytical layer, not build from scratch.
evidence.loss_aversion: traceability and quality; reliable metrics — data reliability is a named concern but framing is delivery-oriented, not compliance or audit-driven.
